pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 3 of 16 Program Overview • Enhance equity returns through an active, value-added approach Role • Drive returns through appreciation, sometimes with leverage; negligible cash yield • Harvest the illiquidity premium • Net asset value (NAV) at $92.1bn as of December 31, 2024 • Buyout at $63.3bn (68.7%), Growth at $21.9bn (23.7%), Opportunistic at $3.9bn (4.3%), Venture at $2.2bn (2.3%), and Credit at $0.9bn (1.0%) Key Metrics • U.S. portfolio at $62.9bn (67.3%), Europe at $20.9bn (22.4%), Emerging Markets at $6.0bn (6.4%), and Developed Asia at $1.2bn (1.3%) • Approximately 133 managers and 394 funds1 • A long investment horizon is a responsibility and an advantage (IB 2) • Long-term value creation requires effective management of three forms of capital: financial, physical and human (IB 4) Investment • Take risk only where we have a strong belief we will be rewarded for it (IB 7) Beliefs • Costs matter and need to be effectively managed (IB 8) • Strong processes and teamwork and deep resources are needed to achieve CalPERS goals and objectives (IB 10) 1 Funds, Fund-of-Funds, and Customized Investment Accounts / Separately Managed Accounts. The manager count includes legacy relationships (i.e., managers with which CalPERS has not recommitted) 3

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 4 of 16 Program Overview • Commitments have been consistent with the target of Commitments by Fiscal Year $15.5bn +/-15% per year for the last four fiscal years As of March 31, 2025 $ in bn • Maintaining a minimum level of commitments is important to avoiding another “lost decade”1 $18 $16.7 $16 $15.6 $15.2 $14.6 • Under its current strategy, CalPERS PE expressed its Target $14 investment view that the 2023-24 vintages were promising by continuing commitments at the level of the 2021 step- $12 $10.1 function increase, which occurred under the prior strategy $10 $9.1 $8 $7.2 • CalPERS PE’s allocation decision also mitigated the $6 proportion it committed in 2021, which is widely regarded $4 as a challenged vintage and a headwind in the portfolio $2 • Commitments to the 2021 vintage (calendar year, $0 not fiscal) totaled $19.5bn FY 18-19 FY 19-20 FY 20-21 FY 21-22 FY 22-23 FY 23-24 FYTD as of Q1-25 • After over three fiscal years of falling short, CalPERS PE achieved its goal of at least 40% co-invest in FY 2022-23, Capital Commitment FY 2023-24, and FYTD 2024-25 because of a new co- invest strategy launched in the middle of FY 2022-23 (Dec. 2022) Source: PEARS (Active Only) as of December 31, 2024. FYTD 2024-25 is net secondary sales to address fund commitment imbalances in Large/Mega Buyout in vintage years 2019-21. The secondary proceeds were invested in secondary purchases and co-investment. 1 Ref. Board meetings on September 19, 2022, and June 20, 2023 4

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 5 of 16 Portfolio Positioning NAV by Strategy Segments Fund Commitments by FY and Strategy Segments1 As of December 31, 2024 100% 4% Credit Related 9% 15% 12% Opportunistic 90% 1.0% 20% 4.3% Venture Capital 80% 2.3% 36% 31% 70% 21% 37% Large/Mega 60% Buyout 50% Growth/Expansion 39.7% 23.7% 40% 19% 33% 30% 55% 56% 20% 29% 25% 10% 0% FY 20-21 FY 21-22 FY 22-23 FY 23-24 Mid Market Buyout Large/Mega Buyout Middle Market Buyout Growth Venture 28.9% • Large Buyout dominated fund commitments until the middle of FY 2022-23, when CalPERS began enhancing the portfolio with strategy diversification • In Nov. 2022, CalPERS began executing a new PE strategy that immediately achieved run-rate diversification across the strategy segments of PE • Buyout commitments went from 91% in FY 2020-21 to 58% in FY 2023-24 • Within Buyout, Large/Mega commitments went from a majority (73%) in FY 2021-22 to a minority (43%) in FY 2023-24 • CalPERS merged the Growth & Innovation platform into PE to address PE’s longtime underinvestment in Growth and Venture • Growth and Venture went from 9% of commitments in FY 2020-21 to 43% in FY 2023-24 • After over a decade hiatus, CalPERS re-entered Venture; despite Venture’s J-curve dynamics, CalPERS is scaling Venture well with a 14.7% 1-year TWR Source: PEARS (Active Only) as of December 31, 2024 - unlagged. NAV stands for Net Asset Value, and TWR stands for time-weighted return 1 Buyout, Growth, and Venture Only. Excludes secondaries and co-investment 5

• Large Buyout dominated fund commitments until the middle of FY 2022-23, when CalPERS began enhancing the portfolio with strategy diversification
• In Nov. 2022, CalPERS began executing a new PE strategy that immediately achieved run-rate diversification across the strategy segments of PE
• Buyout commitments went from 91% in FY 2020-21 to 58% in FY 2023-24
• Within Buyout, Large/Mega commitments went from a majority (73%) in FY 2021-22 to a minority (43%) in FY 2023-24
• CalPERS merged the Growth & Innovation platform into PE to address PE’s longtime underinvestment in Growth and Venture
• Growth and Venture went from 9% of commitments in FY 2020-21 to 43% in FY 2023-24
• After over a decade hiatus, CalPERS re-entered Venture; despite Venture’s J-curve dynamics, CalPERS is scaling Venture well with a 14.7% 1-year TWR
Source: PEARS (Active Only) as of December 31, 2024 - unlagged. NAV stands for Net Asset Value, and TWR stands for time-weighted return
1 Buyout, Growth, and Venture Only. Excludes secondaries and co-investment
5

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 7 of 16 Program Overview Capital Contributions and Distributions As of December 31, 2024 $ in mm 15,000 10,000 5,000 - (5,000) (10,000) (15,000) (20,000) (25,000) 2015 2016 2017 2018 2019 2020 2021 2022 2023 2024 Capital Contributed Total Distributions • CalPERS PE has been cash flow negative as it scales to meet higher asset allocation targets and executes the current PE strategy • In 2021, CalPERS approved increasing the PE target from 8% to 13% • In 2024, CalPERS approved an increase from 13% to 17%, avoiding repeating its past mistakes of procyclicality • Emphasis on secondaries and co-invest has accelerated (1) NAV scaling and (2) the impact of decisions by CalPERS PE on returns Source: PEARS as of December 31, 2023 7

• In 2021, CalPERS approved increasing the PE target from 8% to 13%
• In 2024, CalPERS approved an increase from 13% to 17%, avoiding repeating its past mistakes of procyclicality

Source: PEARS as of December 31, 2023

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 8 of 16 Positioning for the Market Environment Themes Since Nov. 2022: • Commit consistently by vintage year to avoid another lost decade for CalPERS PE from under-allocation and procyclicality • Invest in diverse and emerging managers to improve returns • Engage with investment partners to prioritize sustainable investment frameworks, such as Climate and Labor Principles • Diversify the portfolio with Venture and Growth strategies to reduce Buyout exposure proportionally • Recompose the Buyout portfolio from Mega/Large to Middle Market • Make manager selection the centerpiece of the investment process • Pursue structural alpha opportunities: • Reduce costs by increasing co-investment without management fees or carry to enhance net returns • Employ secondaries as a buyer and seller to optimize the portfolio • Establish separately managed accounts for bespoke opportunities with general partners • Avoid the deployment delay after limited partner decision-making that characterizes traditional Private Equity fund investing • Reduce the ratio of unfunded to NAV to enhance liquidity and positioning to be opportunistic • Maintain a US-centric portfolio while opportunistically investing internationally • Emphasize professional development, team communication, and internal promotions to enhance the 40-person PE Program’s capabilities and culture Current Considerations: • Inflation and interest rates have stabilized at higher levels than persisted for over a decade • Distributions have been well below historical levels 8
pages.raw_text:
Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 8 of 16
Positioning for the Market Environment
Themes Since Nov. 2022:
• Commit consistently by vintage year to avoid another lost decade for CalPERS PE from under-allocation and procyclicality
• Invest in diverse and emerging managers to improve returns
• Engage with investment partners to prioritize sustainable investment frameworks, such as Climate and Labor Principles
• Diversify the portfolio with Venture and Growth strategies to reduce Buyout exposure proportionally
• Recompose the Buyout portfolio from Mega/Large to Middle Market
• Make manager selection the centerpiece of the investment process
• Pursue structural alpha opportunities:
• Reduce costs by increasing co-investment without management fees or carry to enhance net returns
• Employ secondaries as a buyer and seller to optimize the portfolio
• Establish separately managed accounts for bespoke opportunities with general partners
• Avoid the deployment delay after limited partner decision-making that characterizes traditional Private Equity fund investing
• Reduce the ratio of unfunded to NAV to enhance liquidity and positioning to be opportunistic
• Maintain a US-centric portfolio while opportunistically investing internationally
• Emphasize professional development, team communication, and internal promotions to enhance the 40-person PE Program’s capabilities and culture
Current Considerations:
• Inflation and interest rates have stabilized at higher levels than persisted for over a decade
• Distributions have been well below historical levels
8
lines: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 8 of 16
Positioning for the Market Environment
Themes Since Nov. 2022:
• Commit consistently by vintage year to avoid another lost decade for CalPERS PE from under-allocation and procyclicality
• Invest in diverse and emerging managers to improve returns
• Engage with investment partners to prioritize sustainable investment frameworks, such as Climate and Labor Principles
• Diversify the portfolio with Venture and Growth strategies to reduce Buyout exposure proportionally
• Recompose the Buyout portfolio from Mega/Large to Middle Market
• Make manager selection the centerpiece of the investment process
• Pursue structural alpha opportunities:
• Reduce costs by increasing co-investment without management fees or carry to enhance net returns
• Employ secondaries as a buyer and seller to optimize the portfolio
• Establish separately managed accounts for bespoke opportunities with general partners
• Avoid the deployment delay after limited partner decision-making that characterizes traditional Private Equity fund investing
• Reduce the ratio of unfunded to NAV to enhance liquidity and positioning to be opportunistic
• Maintain a US-centric portfolio while opportunistically investing internationally
• Emphasize professional development, team communication, and internal promotions to enhance the 40-person PE Program’s capabilities and culture
Current Considerations:
• Inflation and interest rates have stabilized at higher levels than persisted for over a decade
• Distributions have been well below historical levels

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 9 of 16 Portfolio Performance | Public Equity & Universe TWR Benchmarks Time-weighted Rate of Return (TWR) as of March 31, 2025 • CalPERS PE is outperforming the time- weighted version of the SSPEI in the 1- and 3-year periods 20.0% 18.8% • The CalPERS Private Equity portfolio is outperforming the Policy Benchmark 18.0% (Public Equity with an illiquidity 16.0% premium) for the 5-year period 14.0% 12.8% 13.2% 12.7% 11.3% 11.8% 11.9% 11.3% 12.0% • A surge in the 1-year policy benchmark has not yet been reflected in PE 10.0% valuations, which lag public markets; 8.0% 7.0% 6.8% public-private comparisons should be 5.3% made over longer time periods 6.0% 4.0% 2.6% • CalPERS PE’s improved performance 2.0% versus PE indexes and peers, resulting 0.0% from the current strategy 10 Year 5 Year 3 Year 1 Year implementation, is likely a leading indicator that CalPERS PE returns will Private Equity State Street Private Equity Index (SSPEI) Private Equity Policy Benchmark improve relative to the policy benchmark over the next several years. The next two performance slides cover PE index and peer benchmarking Source: State Street as of March 31, 2025. The CalPERS PE policy benchmark: Custom FTSE All World, All Cap Equity + 150 bps with a quarter lag Notes: The 10-year benchmark number is a composite of linked benchmarks. SSPEI returns are calculated using ModDietz methodology as a proxy for TWR 9

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 12 of 16 Portfolio Performance | Current versus Prior Strategy Breakout of Total CalPERS PE Portfolio between the Current and Prior Strategies • The prior two performance slides show the current strategy’s impact on the total PE portfolio (i.e., the blend of the current and prior TVPI (Non- Weighted Avg. Hold strategies). This slide distills returns between Strategy Cost ($bn) the two strategies annualized) (Years) • Launched in Nov. 2022, the current strategy is Current Strategy (~2023-24) $29 1.24x 0.97 driving the improved performance in the total PE portfolio, the supermajority of which are assets Prior Strategy (~Pre-2023) $109 1.78x 6.01 invested under the prior strategy • Current strategy’s annualized TVPI is meaningfully above that of the prior Invested by Strategy Annualized TVPI: Current vs Prior Strategy strategy (1.25 vs 1.10, respectively) 1.00 1.05 1.10 1.15 1.20 1.25 1.30 • The current strategy accounts for about one-fifth of the capital CalPERS PE has deployed on a cost basis. If the current strategy continues to 21% outperform, the total PE portfolio should Current 1.25x experience continued improvement in returns as the proportion of invested under the current strategy increases • ~25% of the current strategy portfolio is marked at cost since the investments are recent, a 79% Prior 1.10x headwind to returns • TVPI is gross of fees and expenses, so it does Current Prior not consider the more cost-effective implementation of the current strategy Source: PEARS and Staff Analysis as of 12/31/24 Note: Figures are gross of fees and expenses at the company level and include active and exited holdings. TVPI stands for total value to paid-in ratio. Annualized TVPI is calculated using 12 simple annualization

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 13 of 16 Key Initiatives | Cost-effective Implementation Assumptions • Co-investment provides significant cost Committed $1,000,000,000 savings and helps limited partners capture more of Private Equity’s returns Gross multiple 2.5x • Each $1bn in co-invest is expected to save Management fee 1.25% about $400mm in management fees and carry over the life of PE investments under Carry 20% realistic assumptions Fund life 10 • Based on CalPERS annual deployment budget of $15.5bn and a co-invest ratio of 40%, fee savings would be approximately Savings versus fund economics $2.5bn over a 10-year period for each year Management fee $125,000,000 of commitments Carry $275,000,000 • Approximately $25bn of reduced costs for 10 years of commitments Total savings from direct/co-investment $400,000,000 Source: CalPERS Staff Analysis 13

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 14 of 16 Key Initiatives | Integration of Governance and Sustainability Highlights of CalPERS PE’s Efforts over the Last Year to Enhance Returns through Building a More Sustainable Portfolio: • Labor Principles • Coordinated with Sustainable Investments to adopt the Labor Principles and a stakeholder engagement process • Working with PE investment partners to obtain their attestations to operate in broad alignment with the Labor Principles • Scaling back or ending commitments to PE funds that have not been aligning with CalPERS values on human capital • Diversity • Shifting focus to smaller funds in Buyout, expanding Growth, and adding Venture to increase and diversify the opportunity set • In FY 2023-24, committed over $6bn to diverse managers in the non-intermediated portfolio (i.e., not including TPG Next & GCM Elevate) • Co-hosted the second edition of the Catalyst: California’s Emerging and Diverse Investment Manager Forum in May 2025 • Diligence • Incorporated Governance and Sustainability questions into the CalPERS Due Diligence Questionnaire • Coordinating with Sustainable Investments as it further integrates Governance and Sustainability into Private Markets diligence • Encouraging expanded industry participation in the ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI), which CalPERS co-founded • Climate • Working with Sustainable Investments to develop an actionable pipeline contributing to the $100bn 2030 goal • Made primary commitments to several specialized Climate funds and continuing to source fund investment opportunities • Funded about $1.4bn of PE co-investments supporting Climate solutions and continuing to source co-invest opportunities 14
pages.raw_text:
Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 14 of 16
Key Initiatives |
Integration of Governance and Sustainability
Highlights of CalPERS PE’s Efforts over the Last Year to Enhance Returns through Building a More Sustainable Portfolio:
• Labor Principles
• Coordinated with Sustainable Investments to adopt the Labor Principles and a stakeholder engagement process
• Working with PE investment partners to obtain their attestations to operate in broad alignment with the Labor Principles
• Scaling back or ending commitments to PE funds that have not been aligning with CalPERS values on human capital
• Diversity
• Shifting focus to smaller funds in Buyout, expanding Growth, and adding Venture to increase and diversify the opportunity set
• In FY 2023-24, committed over $6bn to diverse managers in the non-intermediated portfolio (i.e., not including TPG Next &
GCM Elevate)
• Co-hosted the second edition of the Catalyst: California’s Emerging and Diverse Investment Manager Forum in May 2025
• Diligence
• Incorporated Governance and Sustainability questions into the CalPERS Due Diligence Questionnaire
• Coordinating with Sustainable Investments as it further integrates Governance and Sustainability into Private Markets diligence
• Encouraging expanded industry participation in the ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI), which CalPERS co-founded
• Climate
• Working with Sustainable Investments to develop an actionable pipeline contributing to the $100bn 2030 goal
• Made primary commitments to several specialized Climate funds and continuing to source fund investment opportunities
• Funded about $1.4bn of PE co-investments supporting Climate solutions and continuing to source co-invest opportunities
14

• Scaling back or ending commitments to PE funds that have not been aligning with CalPERS values on human capital
• Diversity

• In FY 2023-24, committed over $6bn to diverse managers in the non-intermediated portfolio (i.e., not including TPG Next &
GCM Elevate)
• Co-hosted the second edition of the Catalyst: California’s Emerging and Diverse Investment Manager Forum in May 2025
• Diligence
• Incorporated Governance and Sustainability questions into the CalPERS Due Diligence Questionnaire
• Coordinating with Sustainable Investments as it further integrates Governance and Sustainability into Private Markets diligence
• Encouraging expanded industry participation in the ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI), which CalPERS co-founded
• Climate
• Working with Sustainable Investments to develop an actionable pipeline contributing to the $100bn 2030 goal
• Made primary commitments to several specialized Climate funds and continuing to source fund investment opportunities
• Funded about $1.4bn of PE co-investments supporting Climate solutions and continuing to source co-invest opportunities

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 15 of 16 Key Initiatives | Integration of Governance and Sustainability • Since the last PE Annual Program Review, CalPERS co-hosted Emerging and Diverse Managers with CalSTRS the second edition of the Catalyst: California’s Emerging and Diverse Investment Manager Forum, bringing In billions ($) together institutional investors and other global allocators to engage with diverse entrepreneurs and entrepreneurial general $7.0 partners to forge a new path in leadership and growth $6.3 $6.0 • Under its current strategy, CalPERS PE is pursuing alpha through better manager selection. As part of that effort, PE has increased $5.0 $4.1 its commitments to emerging and diverse managers in its non- $4.0 intermediated portfolio (i.e., CalPERS PE’s direct portfolio, which excludes TPG Next and GCM Elevate, both of which select $3.0 managers on behalf of CalPERS under the oversight of $2.0 Sustainable Investments). PE’s emerging and diverse manager $2.0 $1.4 commitments are reported to the California legislature in compliance with California law $1.0 $- • The $1bn partnership with external emerging manager advisors FY 22-23 FY 23-24 TPG Next and GCM Grosvenor is designed to invest over multiple years, is focused on Private Market investments, and is Diverse Managers Emerging Managers overseen by Sustainable Investments Source: As reported in “Assembly Bill 890: Diversity in the Management of Investments,” dated March 1, 2025 (https://www.calpers.ca.gov/documents/assembly-bill- 890-year-three-report-fy-2023-24/download?inline) and in Wilshire’s Consultant Trust Level Review, dated August 31, 2023 15 (https://www.calpers.ca.gov/documents/202309-invest-item05c-03-a/download)

pages.text: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 16 of 16 Key Initiatives | Integration of Governance and Sustainability ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI) • EDCI is a global partnership of private markets stakeholders committed to streamlining the industry’s historically fragmented approach to collecting and reporting ESG data. The mission is to create a critical mass of meaningful, performance-based, and comparable private company sustainability data • CalPERS Private Equity co-founded EDCI in 2021 and serves on the steering committee • EDCI launched with 7 GPs and 9 LPs/IMs representing about $4 trillion in assets under management (AUM) and now has over 350 GPs and 150 LPs/IMs representing about $38 trillion of AUM • As part of its current strategy launched in Q4 2022, CalPERS PE has worked to increase engagement with EDCI, as well as the participation of its active Buyout managers in EDCI The number of GPs participating went from 20 to 42, increasing the engagement rate from 49% to 68% o The proportion of NAV with EDCI-participating GPs went from 50% to 75% o EDCI participation increased even as the current PE strategy added new managers to the portfolio outside o Large Buyout. Many of these Middle Market managers had not been participating in EDCI before partnering with CalPERS Source: CalPERS Staff Analysis and www.esgdc.org 16
pages.raw_text:
Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 16 of 16
Key Initiatives |
Integration of Governance and Sustainability
ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI)
• EDCI is a global partnership of private markets stakeholders committed to streamlining the industry’s historically
fragmented approach to collecting and reporting ESG data. The mission is to create a critical mass of meaningful,
performance-based, and comparable private company sustainability data
• CalPERS Private Equity co-founded EDCI in 2021 and serves on the steering committee
• EDCI launched with 7 GPs and 9 LPs/IMs representing about $4 trillion in assets under management (AUM) and
now has over 350 GPs and 150 LPs/IMs representing about $38 trillion of AUM
• As part of its current strategy launched in Q4 2022, CalPERS PE has worked to increase engagement with EDCI,
as well as the participation of its active Buyout managers in EDCI
The number of GPs participating went from 20 to 42, increasing the engagement rate from 49% to 68%
o
The proportion of NAV with EDCI-participating GPs went from 50% to 75%
o
EDCI participation increased even as the current PE strategy added new managers to the portfolio outside
o
Large Buyout. Many of these Middle Market managers had not been participating in EDCI before partnering
with CalPERS
Source: CalPERS Staff Analysis and www.esgdc.org
16
lines: Private Equity Annual Program Review as of December 31, 2024 Agenda Item 6c, Attachment 1, Page 16 of 16
Key Initiatives |
Integration of Governance and Sustainability
ESG Data Convergence Initiative (EDCI)
• EDCI is a global partnership of private markets stakeholders committed to streamlining the industry’s historically
fragmented approach to collecting and reporting ESG data. The mission is to create a critical mass of meaningful,
performance-based, and comparable private company sustainability data
• CalPERS Private Equity co-founded EDCI in 2021 and serves on the steering committee
• EDCI launched with 7 GPs and 9 LPs/IMs representing about $4 trillion in assets under management (AUM) and
now has over 350 GPs and 150 LPs/IMs representing about $38 trillion of AUM
• As part of its current strategy launched in Q4 2022, CalPERS PE has worked to increase engagement with EDCI,
as well as the participation of its active Buyout managers in EDCI
The number of GPs participating went from 20 to 42, increasing the engagement rate from 49% to 68%
o
The proportion of NAV with EDCI-participating GPs went from 50% to 75%
o
EDCI participation increased even as the current PE strategy added new managers to the portfolio outside
o
Large Buyout. Many of these Middle Market managers had not been participating in EDCI before partnering
with CalPERS
Source: CalPERS Staff Analysis and www.esgdc.org
